title: ICE cotton rises on bullish tone, backed by strong crude oil
summary:
ICE cotton extended gains, with July 2026 closing above 80 cents per pound for the first time since March 2024, supported by strong speculative buying and rising crude oil prices.
Despite lower trading volumes, bullish momentum remains intact with multiple contract highs. Geopolitical tensions and supply concerns continue to drive volatility.

title: US CBP launches phase I of CAPE system for tariff refunds
summary:
The US Customs and Border Protection has launched phase I of the Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries (CAPE) tool in the Automated Commercial Environment Secure Data Portal. 
Importers and customs brokers can file phase 1 requests for refunds through the portal. 
In this phase, CAPE will process most entries that are either unliquidated or up to 80 days past their liquidation date.

title: Spain to host WrapFest 2026 to showcase wrapping innovations
summary:
WrapFest 2026 will unite the wrapping industry with FESPA’s co-located events in Barcelona, offering over 2,000m² of exhibits, live World Wrap Masters competitions, and expert-led WrapTalks. 
The event will showcase innovations, foster cross-sector collaboration, and highlight growth opportunities in vehicle wrapping, print, and personalisation.

title: Kornit Digital debuts Presto MAX PLUS at Texprocess 2026 in Frankfurt
summary:
Kornit Digital unveiled Presto MAX PLUS at Texprocess 2026, expanding digital textile production into footwear, automotive interiors and performance wear. 
The water-free, single-step system eliminates minimum orders, supports diverse fabrics, and integrates DuraTech for durability and Precise Overprint for accuracy, enabling scalable, sustainable and high-performance manufacturing.

title: Argentina key gateway for Vietnamese goods to enter S America: Envoy
summary:
Vietnam will continue to serve as a bridge for Argentina to access the vast ASEAN and Asia-Pacific markets, according to Ngo Minh Nguyet, the former’s ambassador to the latter. 
Argentina remains a strategic gateway for Vietnamese goods to enter the South American markets, she said. 
Negotiations commenced in December last on a preferential trade agreement between Vietnam and the MERCOSUR, she said.

title: Germany’s producer prices fall annually in March, rise monthly
summary:
Germany’s producer prices fell 0.2 per cent YoY in March 2026 but rose 2.5 per cent MoM, the sharpest increase since August 2022, driven by energy volatility. 
Energy prices declined annually but surged monthly, led by mineral oil products. 
Excluding energy, prices rose moderately. 
Capital and durable goods increased, while non-durable consumer goods recorded a slight annual decline.

title: Mayer & Cie stays a strong and trusted brand, restarts operations
summary:
Mayer & Cie resumes operations under new ownership led by Xu Hongjie, focusing on customer-centric, premium circular knitting solutions. 
Retaining its Albstadt base, the company blends German engineering with agile management, aims to restore profitability, streamline costs, and reclaim global leadership, backed by an experienced returning team.

title: Asia faces uneven inflation, growth risks amid energy shock: Nomura
summary:
Asian economies face uneven inflation and growth impacts from the US–Iran energy shock, with emerging markets more vulnerable due to weaker fiscal, energy and FX buffers, according to Nomura. 
Supply disruptions may persist despite a ceasefire, pressuring growth. 
Inflation effects vary amid subsidy policies. 
Policy tightening is likely in select economies, while others may remain accommodative.

title: China textile & apparel exports edge up to $67 bn in Q1 2026
summary:
China’s textile and apparel exports rose 1.21 per cent to $67.08 billion in Q1 2026, signalling a steady recovery after a weak 2025.
Textile shipments grew 2.8 per cent, while garments edged down 0.4 per cent.
A sharp 17.3 per cent rise in yarn and fabric imports reflects raw material restocking by mills amid improving demand and stabilising global trade conditions.

title: Global energy growth slows to 1.3% in 2025: Report
summary:
Global energy demand growth slowed to 1.3 per cent in 2025, while electricity demand rose around 3 per cent, driven by EVs, industry, and data centres, according to IEA. 
Solar PV led supply growth for the first time. 
Oil demand grew modestly, and coal growth slowed. 
CO2 emissions rose slightly. 
Renewables and nuclear expansion highlighted an accelerating shift towards cleaner energy systems.

title: Denmark’s apparel imports see sharp drop in January 2026
summary:
Denmark’s apparel imports saw a sharp YoY correction, signalling demand slowdown after strong 2025 growth.
Core categories showed resilience in share, indicating stable baseline consumption despite lower volumes.
Steeper decline in outerwear reflects weaker discretionary spending and possible seasonal effects.
The drop likely points to inventory correction and cautious retail sentiment.

title: US AAFA urges CARB to align with existing emissions reporting outline
summary:
AAFA, responding to the California Air Resources Board's (CARB) request for feedback on GHG emissions reporting requirements under SB 253, urged CARB to align with existing emissions reporting frameworks and ensure flexibility in the use of standards. 
AAFA recommended that CARB not mandate specific methods and allow flexibility to report using whichever method is the best fit for the reporting entity.

title: UK’s clothing exports down 7.6% to $293 mn in Jan-Feb
summary:
UK's clothing exports declined 7.66 per cent to £217 million (~$293 million) in February 2026, amid weak European demand and cost pressures. 
Textile and fibre exports also fell year on year, though month-on-month trends show mild recovery. 
Full-year 2025 exports remained under pressure, extending a post-pandemic correction. 
Q4 data indicates only a partial sequential rebound.

title: UK’s apparel imports increase 2.3% to $1.78 bn in Feb 2026
summary:
The UK’s clothing imports rose 2.32 per cent year on year (YoY) to £1.320 billion (~$1.789 billion) in February 2026, with a marginal month on month (MoM) increase. 
Fabric imports edged up, while fibre imports remained flat. 
Despite softer quarterly trends in late 2025, full-year 2025 apparel imports grew over 6 per cent, signalling resilient demand amid mixed textile segment performance.

title: Italy’s OVS’ FY25 sales rise 7% to $2.06 bn; beats market
summary:
OVS has posted record FY25 sales of €1,745.9 million (~$2.06 billion), up 7 per cent YoY, driven by like-for-like growth and Goldenpoint consolidation. 
Adjusted gross margin rose 8.8 per cent, while net profit increased 14.8 per cent. 
Key brands delivered solid EBITDA gains. 
Womenswear and beauty led growth, with early FY26 performance remaining strong on robust collection demand.

title: Revoking China PNTR may lead to higher tariffs borne by US firms: AAFA
summary:
US trade body AAFA has urged the International Trade Commission not to revoke the permanent normal trade relations (PNTR) status granted to China as that would result in higher tariffs borne by US companies. 
Higher tariffs on Chinese imports would constrain US firms’ ability to invest in innovation, expand operations and support US job growth, and would risk closing off commercial opportunities in China.

title: India’s T&A exports fall 14% in March, FY26 down 2.21%
summary:
India’s textile and apparel exports fell sharply in March 2026, declining 14.02 per cent year on year (YoY) to $2.91 billion amid geopolitical disruptions. 
Apparels dropped 18.99 per cent and textiles 9.91 per cent. 
FY 2025–26 exports slipped 2.21 per cent, with sector share easing to 8.10 per cent. 
Cotton imports surged annually, while MMF trends remained relatively stable.

title: US CBP to soon launch electronic system for importers to claim refunds
summary:
US Customs and Border Protection will launch on April 20 an electronic system that importers can use to claim tariff refunds authorised by court order or applicable law. 
Phase 1 will be limited to certain unliquidated entries and certain entries within 80 days of liquidation.
Refunds will be issued within 60-90 days of the Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries declaration getting accepted.

title: Bangladesh RMG units call for allowing local FOC raw material sourcing
summary:
Bangladesh RMG players have urged the government to remove restrictions on sourcing raw materials free of cost (FOC) from local suppliers. 
Industry leaders said allowing FOC sourcing from domestic suppliers would boost local sales, strengthen backward linkage industries and raise overall value addition in the export-oriented sector. 
Many local suppliers can provide inputs now against global orders.

title: UK’s Sosandar returns to profitability amid robust FY26 performance
summary:
Sosandar reported FY26 revenue of £42.3 million (~$57.53 million), up 14 per cent, driven by strong online growth, with own-site sales rising 24 per cent. 
The company returned to profitability with PBT of £0.4 million (~$0.54 million) and improved margins. 
Despite slightly missing revenue expectations, performance remained solid. 
Strong third-party sales supported confidence in profitable growth.
